records.ety: [F. conomique, L. oeconomicus orderly, methodical, Gr. <?/ economical. See Economy.]

defs: Relating to domestic economy, or to the management of household affairs.
quotes: And doth employ her economic art And busy care, her household to preserve.

defs: Managing with frugality; guarding against waste or unnecessary expense; careful and frugal in management and in expenditure; -- said of character or habits.
quotes: Just rich enough, with economic care, To save a pittance.

defs: Managed with frugality; not marked with waste or extravagance; using the minimum of time or effort or resources required for effectiveness; frugal; -- said of acts; saving; as, an economical use of money or of time; an economic use of home heating oil.

of or pertaining to the national or regional economy; relating to political economy; relating to the means of living, or the resources and wealth of a country; relating to the production or consumption of goods and services of a nation or region; as, economic growth; economic purposes; economical truths; an economic downturn.
quotes: There was no economical distress in England to prompt the enterprises of colonization.
Economic questions, such as money, usury, taxes, lands, and the employment of the people.

defs: Regulative; relating to the adaptation of means to an end.
avoiding waste; as, an economical meal. Opposite of wasteful.
notes: ☞ Economical is the usual form when meaning frugal, saving; economic is the form commonly used when meaning pertaining to the management of a household, or of public affairs.
